stem: RAM is described as volatile memory.
model_answer: Volatile means the memory's contents are lost when electrical power is removed. This is why any unsaved work held only in RAM disappears when the computer is switched off.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Define the term volatile as it applies to computer memory.

mark_points.text: the memory's contents are lost when power is removed
accepted_alternatives: data disappears when power is off

mark_points.text: the memory requires a continuous power supply to retain its data
